A, an associate professor, in the college applied for long leave. The principal of the college proposed to sanction the leave only if she promised in writing that she would not return to job and take ‘VRS’. After completion of leave period A returned to job and gave her joining report which was not accepted by the principal. Advise A as to her rights.

‘A’ goes to a hotel for a dinner. He finds a mobile phone near his table and deposits it with ‘B’ the hotel manager. In spite of vigorous efforts, the owner of the mobile phone could not be traced. ‘A’ requests ‘B’ to return the mobile phone to him, but B refuses to do so. Now, A files a suit against B for getting back the mobile phone. Will he succeed? Give reasons
Ordinary damages are the damages, which are awarded for compensating the loss that arises naturally from the breach of contract. State with reasons whether the statement is true or false.
A draws a cheque payable to B, the bearer. C steals the cheque. Can C demand the payment from the bank or from A or B?
